human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for rights-and-clearances-specific workflow grounding, source authority, records-governance discipline, scenario coverage, refusal boundaries, deployment notes, commercialization notes, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to rights and clearances records, documentation, evidence-package progression, and completion handling for broadcast, film, and streaming production services.

Authority sources cover Copyright Office search, registration, and recordation evidence; WIPO film-rights workflow; SAG-AFTRA performer workpapers; MLC music-rights data boundaries; USPTO title and trademark boundary guidance; MovieLabs/EMA avails; and ISO records-management controls.

Evaluation scenarios cover accepted intake to completion, missing chain-of-title evidence, fair-use refusal, archive and music clearances, missing completion evidence, license negotiation handoff, rights-window conflicts, rework exceptions, generic framing rejection, title and trademark routing, tenant adaptation, and conflicting-source escalation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/broadcast-film-and-streaming-production-services/rights-and-clearances-specialist/ so rights and clearances stays separate from legal, business affairs, music supervision, E&O, title, trademark, standards-and-practices, and distribution approval authority.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained explicit around legal sufficiency, fair use, license negotiation, title or trademark opinion, E&O certification, spend approval, and completion without evidence.
Lifecycle transitions are reversible when evidence fails because blocked, rework, exception, escalated, and complete states are all documented.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated rights and clearances specialist spec pack for evidence-backed rights documentation and safe escalation in broadcast, film, and streaming production services.

Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and evaluation/functionality-map.json, and runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
